narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter April 1st with a high-ceiling core, anchored by Trea Turner and Kyle Schwarber, who both benefit from favorable platoon advantages and prime spots in the order against Cade Cavalli. The model is particularly bullish on Colt Keith today; despite facing a formidable arm in Zac Gallen, his projected #3 spot and +10% platoon boost elevate him to a must-start status. Similarly, Christian Yelich provides a reliable floor against Drew Rasmussen, as his ability to capitalize on right-handed pitching remains a cornerstone for our daily 5x5 Roto efficiency.

Caution is advised regarding Yordan Alvarez, whose score is severely depressed (47.33) due to a brutal matchup against Garrett Crochet; the left-on-left disadvantage (-15%) and the pitcher’s elite skill profile force us to temper expectations. Elsewhere, our "BvP" indicators offer a mixed bag: Turner’s small-sample success against Cavalli provides a nice tailwind, while Schwarber must overcome a poor 6-plate-appearance history against the same arm. We will monitor the lineup cards closely, as Victor Scott II’s projection is heavily reliant on his speed-based matchup against Freddy Peralta; should he slide out of the #9 spot, his value will dip further, making him our primary candidate for a late-swap if bench options surface.
